name: Children's Literature
description: Classic children's books, fairy tales, and their creators

questions.q: Who wrote 'Alice's Adventures in Wonderland'?

source.notes: Carroll was the pen name of Oxford mathematician Charles Dodgson. Published 1865.

questions.q: In the title of Dodie Smith's novel, how many Dalmatians are there?

source.notes: Originally published in 1956; adapted into the 1961 Disney film.

questions.q: Who wrote the 1900 novel 'The Wonderful Wizard of Oz'?

source.notes: Full name Lyman Frank Baum; wrote 13 further Oz novels.

questions.q: Complete the title of this A.A. Milne classic: 'Winnie the ___'.

source.notes: First published 1926.

questions.q: Complete the title of Roald Dahl's 1964 classic: 'Charlie and the ___'.

source.notes: Sequel 'Charlie and the Great Glass Elevator' published 1972.

questions.q: Complete the title of the Robert Louis Stevenson pirate adventure: 'Treasure ___'.

source.notes: Published 1883; introduced Long John Silver.

questions.q: In 'Cinderella', what does the Fairy Godmother turn a pumpkin into?

source.notes: Mice become horses, a rat becomes a coachman.

questions.q: Who created the beloved character Peter Rabbit, alongside Flopsy, Mopsy, and Cotton-tail?

source.notes: First privately published 1901; commercially released 1902.

questions.q: Which British author created the marmalade-loving bear Paddington in 1958?

source.notes: Paddington came from 'darkest Peru'.

questions.q: Who wrote the children's novel 'War Horse', later adapted into a film by Steven Spielberg?

source.notes: Published 1982; Spielberg's adaptation released 2011.

questions.q: Who wrote 'Charlie and the Chocolate Factory', 'Matilda', and 'The BFG'?

source.notes: Roald Dahl (1916–1990) is one of the best-selling children's authors of all time.

questions.q: Who wrote the 'Mary Poppins' series of novels?

source.notes: First book published 1934; Disney film released 1964.

questions.q: Who wrote the 1911 novel 'The Secret Garden'?

source.notes: Also wrote 'A Little Princess' and 'Little Lord Fauntleroy'.

questions.q: Which British author wrote both 'The Famous Five' and 'The Secret Seven' book series?

source.notes: Blyton wrote more than 700 books.

questions.q: In Rudyard Kipling's 'The Jungle Book', what kind of animal is Baloo?

source.notes: Bagheera is the black panther and Shere Khan the tiger.

questions.q: In 'Snow White', what kind of food does the Evil Queen use to put Snow White into a deathlike sleep?

source.notes: A poisoned apple; the Disney version shows her dipping it into a cauldron.

questions.q: The classic fairy tales 'Hansel and Gretel' and 'Rapunzel' were compiled and retold by how many 'Brothers Grimm'?

source.notes: Jacob (1785–1863) and Wilhelm (1786–1859) Grimm.

questions.q: In Pixar's 'Ratatouille', what is the name of the rat who dreams of being a chef?

source.notes: Voiced by Patton Oswalt; co-protagonist with Linguini.

questions.q: Disney's 'Frozen' is loosely based on which Hans Christian Andersen tale?

source.notes: Andersen's original tale published 1844.

questions.q: In which region of England did children's author Beatrix Potter live for most of her life?

source.notes: Potter left 4,000 acres to the National Trust, helping preserve the Lake District.

questions.q: In Hans Christian Andersen's fairy tale, how tall is the tiny heroine Thumbelina?

source.notes: Born from a magic barleycorn, about the size of a thumb.

questions.q: What is the first name of Hugh Lofting's animal-talking character 'Doctor Dolittle'?

source.notes: Dr. John Dolittle; first book published 1920.
